title: My live 0DTE SPY scalper on the Public API -here's the actual execution engine behind my dashboard
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
Here's what's actually running on one of the systems underneath the dashboard I posted here: 

[https://www.reddit.com/r/ai\_trading/comments/1rcmtuu/built\_three\_trading\_systems\_scans\_executes/](https://www.reddit.com/r/ai_trading/comments/1rcmtuu/built_three_trading_systems_scans_executes/)



**The setup**

Every 30 seconds during market hours, this runs:

ES price → VWAP σ-band scan → SPY 0DTE chain → signal fires → order placed → fill monitored → stop or target

Entry trigger: ES futures price touches a 3σ VWAP band. The system scans SPY 0DTE options priced $0.50–$3.00, picks the directional contract, and submits via Public API. Why I use SPY over SPX? 10x cheaper commissions. Tighter spreads and SPY generates rebate revenue where SPX doesn't.

Risk rules are hardcoded:

* 50% stop loss (enter $0.53 → stop at $0.27)
* 100% gain target (exit at $1.06)
* VWAP reversal exit — if price crosses back through the band, close immediately
* Hard close at 3:59 PM regardless



**The actual API call**

    order = client.place_option_order(
        osi_symbol="SPY260304P00682000",
        side=OrderSide.BUY,
        quantity=1,
        order_type=OrderType.LIMIT,
        limit_price=0.53
    )
    

That's pretty much it. Full options chain access, real-time quotes, multi-leg support - all through REST API. The scalper enters on limit orders - means instead of paying the spread, I'm capturing it. Public pays $0.06/contract rebate at base tier, up to $0.10 at higher volume. Small per trade but real at scale  whether the trade is winner or looser



**Today's log (live)**

    SPY 682P — entry $0.53 → stop hit $0.26 — P&L: -$27
    SPY 677C — entry $0.62 → target hit $1.58 — P&L: +$96
    SPY 682P — entry $0.53 → stop $0.26 — P&L: -$27
    

Not every trade works. The edge is in the structure: defined risk, asymmetric exits, rebates on every fill. 





https://preview.redd.it/8vdw8tmg04ng1.png?width=1000&format=png&auto=webp&s=cfb928cfbf22096db009dc3a89a0753b6ef0fb51

https://preview.redd.it/j6wqd1qh04ng1.png?width=1000&format=png&auto=webp&s=80a5d20036441a3e81765d6b3d81ecc309f1e0ac

comments.author: wado729
comments.body: Is Public API free?  Why Public instead of Interactive Brokers?

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body:
The public api is free to use just pull a key from your account. I trade options on public over IBKR because I have zero fee drag- rebates at 10c/contract. So when I’m trading more volume and especially when I scale up, the commission factor my equity curve is not hurting the performance. If you trade a lot and you have scratch trades, strategies live and die by fee structure. 

The API is much easier to work with- almost zero downtime. With IBKR I’m constantly managing feed disconnects that can throw models off.
